title: Ord Namurt

'''Ord Namurt''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and the site of an [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]]. [[The Dark Woman|An'ya Kuro]], known as the Dark Woman, had a [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] ground for [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|students]] on the planet. She was training [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] on this [[Planet/Legends|planet]] when the girl was kidnapped by star [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]].

==History==
{{Quote|We never really connected. When Aurra was kidnapped by star pirates on that trip to Ord Namurt, I decided to let her go. It was the will of the Force, I told myself.|An'ya Kuro|<ref name="AS">''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]''</ref>}}
Ord Namurt was a planet in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]<ref name="EAOC" />, located in the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]], a densely populated region of space and a crossroads of numerous [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] near [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]. Around [[13,000 BBY]], when the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], represented by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], were taking their first steps in exploring the worlds on the galactic frontier, Ord Namurt was chosen for the construction of military and reconnaissance bases on the planet, establishing a foothold for the Republic in the region. Each such installation was designated as a [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] (ORD), a title that would be preserved in the planet’s name for millennia.<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

In [[11,987 BBY]], the [[Pius Dea]] theocratic sect, which adhered to [[Humanocentrism|anthropocentric]] views, seized power in the Republic. Just [[11,920 BBY|over half a century later]], during the [[Pius Dea Crusades]] against [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species, the cult launched its third invasion of Hutt Space, known as the [[Fourth Pius Dea Crusade]]. During the war, the cult’s forces struck [[Delacrix]] from Ord Namurt, passing through the planets [[Vaathkree]] and [[Klatooine]].<ref name="EW" />

[[File:JediFamilyPortrait-SWR29.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Aurra Sing was training on Ord Namurt when she was kidnapped by star pirates]]
Around [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[An'ya Kuro]], known as the Dark Woman, had a [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] ground for [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|students]] on the planet<ref name="POTJ>''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref>. She was training [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] on this planet when the girl was kidnapped by star [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], during a trip to Ord Namurt.<ref name="DB">{{DB|character|aurrasing|Sing, Aurra}}</ref>

Near [[34 BBY/Legends|34 BBY]], [[Trench/Legends|Trench]], while in the employ of the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]], led the destruction of a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] base on the planet.<ref name="EW" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet was called "Ord Manurt" in the ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'', and misspelled as "Ord Namur" in ''[[Jedi: Aayla Secura]]''.
